config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse, censorship, and unfair treatment. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first, while still allowing limited restrictions only when they are truly necessary to prevent harm to others. From my perspective, once the state or the majority gets too much power over the individual, ordinary people are the ones who suffer most.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu batas yang mencegah mayoritas menindas minoritas atau negara melampaui wewenangnya. Tentu ada situasi ketika pembatasan terbatas masuk akal, seperti untuk mencegah bahaya nyata bagi orang lain, tetapi beban pembuktiannya harus sangat tinggi. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari individu yang dilindungi, bukan individu yang dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in Indonesia we already know how easily “for the public good” can become a tool for pressure, corruption, and silence. At the same time, I do not believe rights are absolute in a way that ignores real communal responsibilities, because in a country as diverse and crowded as ours, one person’s freedom can genuinely affect many others. So for me the right balance is not society first or individual first, but strong individual rights with narrow, clearly justified limits when there is real harm. If the majority gets too much power, it is usually ordinary people, not elites, who pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang Amerika, frasa itu terlalu sering dipakai untuk melegitimasi kekuasaan yang berlebihan. Argumen tentang tanggung jawab komunal memang valid, tetapi itu tidak boleh berubah menjadi alasan untuk mengorbankan kebebasan berbicara, kebebasan beragama, atau perlindungan dari tindakan sewenang-wenang oleh negara maupun mayoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru bergantung pada hak individu yang jelas dan tegas, dengan pembatasan hanya ketika ada bahaya nyata dan bisa dibuktikan. Kalau tidak, “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu mudah menjadi kedok untuk menekan orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia that idea can quickly turn into pressure from the majority, the state, or local power holders. We do have a strong communal culture, and I respect that, but I have seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence dissent or excuse unfair treatment. A society is healthier when it protects individual rights first and only limits them in narrow cases where there is clear harm to others. If we give society the first claim on everything, the people with the least power are usually the ones who lose.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Amerika, “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering menjadi bahasa halus untuk memperluas kekuasaan negara, menekan minoritas, atau membungkam kritik, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas otomatis seperti itu. Memang ada kewajiban sosial dan pembatasan yang sah ketika ada bahaya nyata bagi orang lain, tetapi beban pembuktiannya harus sangat tinggi dan sangat sempit. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang melindungi hak individu agar kekuasaan tidak mudah disalahgunakan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
